---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: f7f1bdf8ba0fa97f8b8feaecaf64dd1641549b82
findings:
  - { id: judged-quarantine-disclosure-met-ac, kind: judged, text: "ac-2's disclosure is implemented only for unmet ACs: quarantineDisclosures (cmd/verdi/closuregate.go:179) skips any AC whose status is evidenced or waived, so a quarantined record naming a met AC is excluded from the fold with no per-record disclosed-unproven line at all. The spec's language is unconditional per record (\"reads a quarantined record as a per-record disclosed-unproven against the acceptance criterion it would have evidenced\"), and the gap has teeth for adverse evidence: an AC with a reachable current pass record plus a current fail record read violated under fold.go's any-current-fail rule before this story; once the fail record's source commit becomes unreachable it is quarantined, the AC flips to evidenced, and the closure gate passes with zero disclosure — the fail record vanishes silently on exactly the surface whose honesty ac-2 hardens (\"silence is never a pass\"). (confidence 0.50)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Within ac-2's letter but the SHARPEST form of the disclosure-extension residual yet: the met-AC skip can hide a quarantine-caused violated→evidenced flip on the closure surface itself. SCHEDULED for the endgame fix wave (disclose quarantined records against met ACs, at minimum when they carried fail) as part of the queued disclosure-extension work item." }
  - { id: judged-merge-gate-quarantine-silence, kind: judged, text: "The quarantine exclusion lives in the shared loader (evidence.LoadRecordsWithSources), so it applies to every fold consumer, but only the closure gate and close --preflight render quarantine disclosures. The merge gate's not-violated condition (gate.go's checkNoACViolated, which uses the same foldStoryEvidence) silently flips violated -\u003e not-violated when a current fail record's provenance.commit becomes unreachable: before this story that shape surfaced loudly (operational hard-fail); now the adverse record is excluded with no disclosure of any kind on the merge-gate surface. The spec's ac-2 names only the closure gate, so mechanically nothing is violated — but the intent (\"the gate's own verdict stays honest\", exclusion always paired with disclosure) does not carry to the other gate the same fold feeds. (confidence 0.35)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Continuation of the standing sibling-surfaces ruling (previously keyed judged-quarantine-exclusion-still-silent-…, orphaned by slug drift): disclosure-surface-only gap, queued for the endgame's coherent all-surface extension." }
  - { id: judged-undecodable-per-spec-disclosure-commitdir, kind: judged, text: "sync's per-key-class honesty fix classifies an undecodable fetched record file as \"disclosed at closure\" on the first path segment alone (classifyUndecodableKeys: prefix \"spec--\"), but the closure-side walk it cites (evidence.QuarantinedRecords) only re-surfaces RecordFileNames sitting directly under an immediate commit-shaped subdirectory (commitDirRe ^[0-9a-f]{7,40}$). A fetched artifact carrying an undecodable verdicts.json/runtime.json under a per-spec key whose middle segment is not commit-shaped (or is nested deeper) is kept on disk and gets sync's notice promising \"excluded from the fold and disclosed at closure\" — a disclosure that will never fire, the exact claim-accuracy defect finding 3's fix was for, surviving at the key-shape boundary. (confidence 0.35)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Message-accuracy family (the per-key-class notice promises a closure disclosure that cannot fire for non-commit-shaped middle segments): SCHEDULED for the endgame fix wave alongside the disclosure extension; sync-time notice remains the honest record meanwhile." }
digest: sha256:17040954b0a5e26e6d3c15093212c5c16b2db119055b939d6a76ba91e0d9fbbc
integrity: sha256:e31e1c82e5b414c5579672cf68856cc2dfbb2b540f627de9e8c0047ac27c6aec
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"quarantine-disclosure-met-ac\",\"text\":\"ac-2's disclosure is implemented only for unmet ACs: quarantineDisclosures (cmd/verdi/closuregate.go:179) skips any AC whose status is evidenced or waived, so a quarantined record naming a met AC is excluded from the fold with no per-record disclosed-unproven line at all. The spec's language is unconditional per record (\\\"reads a quarantined record as a per-record disclosed-unproven against the acceptance criterion it would have evidenced\\\"), and the gap has teeth for adverse evidence: an AC with a reachable current pass record plus a current fail record read violated under fold.go's any-current-fail rule before this story; once the fail record's source commit becomes unreachable it is quarantined, the AC flips to evidenced, and the closure gate passes with zero disclosure — the fail record vanishes silently on exactly the surface whose honesty ac-2 hardens (\\\"silence is never a pass\\\").\",\"confidence\":0.5},{\"id\":\"merge-gate-quarantine-silence\",\"text\":\"The quarantine exclusion lives in the shared loader (evidence.LoadRecordsWithSources), so it applies to every fold consumer, but only the closure gate and close --preflight render quarantine disclosures. The merge gate's not-violated condition (gate.go's checkNoACViolated, which uses the same foldStoryEvidence) silently flips violated -\u003e not-violated when a current fail record's provenance.commit becomes unreachable: before this story that shape surfaced loudly (operational hard-fail); now the adverse record is excluded with no disclosure of any kind on the merge-gate surface. The spec's ac-2 names only the closure gate, so mechanically nothing is violated — but the intent (\\\"the gate's own verdict stays honest\\\", exclusion always paired with disclosure) does not carry to the other gate the same fold feeds.\",\"confidence\":0.35},{\"id\":\"undecodable-per-spec-disclosure-commitdir\",\"text\":\"sync's per-key-class honesty fix classifies an undecodable fetched record file as \\\"disclosed at closure\\\" on the first path segment alone (classifyUndecodableKeys: prefix \\\"spec--\\\"), but the closure-side walk it cites (evidence.QuarantinedRecords) only re-surfaces RecordFileNames sitting directly under an immediate commit-shaped subdirectory (commitDirRe ^[0-9a-f]{7,40}$). A fetched artifact carrying an undecodable verdicts.json/runtime.json under a per-spec key whose middle segment is not commit-shaped (or is nested deeper) is kept on disk and gets sync's notice promising \\\"excluded from the fold and disclosed at closure\\\" — a disclosure that will never fire, the exact claim-accuracy defect finding 3's fix was for, surviving at the key-shape boundary.\",\"confidence\":0.35}]}\n```" }
frozen: { at: 2026-07-21, commit: f7f1bdf8ba0fa97f8b8feaecaf64dd1641549b82 }

---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-quarantine-disclosure-met-ac** [accepted-deviation]: ac-2's disclosure is implemented only for unmet ACs: quarantineDisclosures (cmd/verdi/closuregate.go:179) skips any AC whose status is evidenced or waived, so a quarantined record naming a met AC is excluded from the fold with no per-record disclosed-unproven line at all. The spec's language is unconditional per record ("reads a quarantined record as a per-record disclosed-unproven against the acceptance criterion it would have evidenced"), and the gap has teeth for adverse evidence: an AC with a reachable current pass record plus a current fail record read violated under fold.go's any-current-fail rule before this story; once the fail record's source commit becomes unreachable it is quarantined, the AC flips to evidenced, and the closure gate passes with zero disclosure — the fail record vanishes silently on exactly the surface whose honesty ac-2 hardens ("silence is never a pass"). (confidence 0.50) — Within ac-2's letter but the SHARPEST form of the disclosure-extension residual yet: the met-AC skip can hide a quarantine-caused violated→evidenced flip on the closure surface itself. SCHEDULED for the endgame fix wave (disclose quarantined records against met ACs, at minimum when they carried fail) as part of the queued disclosure-extension work item.
- **judged-merge-gate-quarantine-silence** [accepted-deviation]: The quarantine exclusion lives in the shared loader (evidence.LoadRecordsWithSources), so it applies to every fold consumer, but only the closure gate and close --preflight render quarantine disclosures. The merge gate's not-violated condition (gate.go's checkNoACViolated, which uses the same foldStoryEvidence) silently flips violated -> not-violated when a current fail record's provenance.commit becomes unreachable: before this story that shape surfaced loudly (operational hard-fail); now the adverse record is excluded with no disclosure of any kind on the merge-gate surface. The spec's ac-2 names only the closure gate, so mechanically nothing is violated — but the intent ("the gate's own verdict stays honest", exclusion always paired with disclosure) does not carry to the other gate the same fold feeds. (confidence 0.35) — Continuation of the standing sibling-surfaces ruling (previously keyed judged-quarantine-exclusion-still-silent-…, orphaned by slug drift): disclosure-surface-only gap, queued for the endgame's coherent all-surface extension.
- **judged-undecodable-per-spec-disclosure-commitdir** [accepted-deviation]: sync's per-key-class honesty fix classifies an undecodable fetched record file as "disclosed at closure" on the first path segment alone (classifyUndecodableKeys: prefix "spec--"), but the closure-side walk it cites (evidence.QuarantinedRecords) only re-surfaces RecordFileNames sitting directly under an immediate commit-shaped subdirectory (commitDirRe ^[0-9a-f]{7,40}$). A fetched artifact carrying an undecodable verdicts.json/runtime.json under a per-spec key whose middle segment is not commit-shaped (or is nested deeper) is kept on disk and gets sync's notice promising "excluded from the fold and disclosed at closure" — a disclosure that will never fire, the exact claim-accuracy defect finding 3's fix was for, surviving at the key-shape boundary. (confidence 0.35) — Message-accuracy family (the per-key-class notice promises a closure disclosure that cannot fire for non-commit-shaped middle segments): SCHEDULED for the endgame fix wave alongside the disclosure extension; sync-time notice remains the honest record meanwhile.

### Candidates awaiting reaffirmation

(none)

(none)
